bibliographic_data.title: Large Scale Evolution of Convolutional Neural Networks Using Volunteer Computing
bibliographic_data.abstract: This work presents a new algorithm called evolutionary exploration of augmenting convolutional topologies (EXACT), which is capable of evolving the structure of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). EXACT is in part modeled after the neuroevolution of augmenting topologies (NEAT) algorithm, with notable exceptions to allow it to scale to large scale distributed computing environments and evolve networks with convolutional filters. In addition to multithreaded and MPI versions, EXACT has been implemented as part of a BOINC volunteer computing project, allowing large scale evolution. During a period of two months, over 4,500 volunteered computers on the Citizen Science Grid trained over 120,000 CNNs and evolved networks reaching 98.32% test data accuracy on the MNIST handwritten digits dataset.

core_content.novelty_claim: EXACT is a new algorithm that evolves the structure of CNNs, capable of scaling to large distributed computing environments and achieving high accuracy on the MNIST dataset.
core_content.key_methodology: EXACT uses an asynchronous evolution strategy with mutation and crossover operations to evolve filter sizes and their connections in CNNs. It has been implemented as part of a BOINC volunteer computing project.
outcomes: Evolved networks achieved 98.32% test data accuracy on the MNIST dataset using rudimentary training strategies.
The evolved CNNs showed improved training and test data accuracy compared to human-designed CNNs without max pooling.
core_content.full_text_markdown: This paper introduces EXACT, an algorithm for evolving the structure of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) that can scale to large distributed computing environments. The algorithm uses an asynchronous evolution strategy with mutation and crossover operations on filter sizes and their connections. It has been implemented in a BOINC volunteer computing project, allowing over 4,500 computers to train and evolve CNNs for two months. The evolved networks achieved high accuracy (98.32%) on the MNIST dataset using simple training strategies, outperforming human-designed architectures.

nodes.label: Evolutionary Exploration of Augmenting Convolutional Topologies
